channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Feds mum on how Laken Riley's killer got one-way plane ticket from migrant shelter ground zero
item.description: The FBI and ICE declined to provide more information about a "humanitarian flight" that Laken Riley's killer, Jose Ibarra, was granted from New York City to Atlanta in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chuck Woolery, smooth-talking game show host of 'Love Connection' and 'Scrabble,' dies at 83
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Chuck Woolery, the affable, smooth-talking game show host of “Wheel of Fortune,” “Love Connection” and “Scrabble” who later became a right-wing podcaster, skewering liberals and accusing the government of lying about COVID-19, has died. He was 83.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Israel cracks down on Palestinian citizens who speak out against the war in Gaza
item.description: UMM AL-FAHM, Israel (AP) — Israel’s yearlong crackdown against Palestinian citizens who speak out against the war in Gaza is prompting many to self-censor out of fear of being jailed and further marginalized in society, while some still find ways to dissent — carefully.
item.dc:creator: SAM MCNEIL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Romanians cast ballots in presidential race that could pit nationalist against leftist in a runoff
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romanians are casting ballots on Sunday in the first round of a presidential election that could pit a far-right nationalist against the incumbent leftist prime minister in the runoff.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Philippine vice president publicly threatens to have the president assassinated
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte said Saturday she has contracted an assassin to kill the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker if she herself is killed, in a brazen public threat that she warned was not a joke.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Fred Harris, former US senator from Oklahoma and presidential hopeful, dies at 94
item.description: Fred Harris, a former U.S. senator from Oklahoma, presidential hopeful and populist who championed Democratic Party reforms in the turbulent 1960s, died Saturday. He was 94. Harris’ wife, Margaret Elliston, confirmed his death to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Civil Air Patrol plane crash during Colorado training exercise leaves 2 dead, 1 injured
item.description: A Civil Air Patrol Cessna 182 crashed in a mountainous area of Larimer County, Colorado, on Saturday. Two people were killed and one was "severely injured.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump taps Rollins as agriculture chief, completing proposed slate of Cabinet secretaries
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he will nominate former White House aide Brooke Rollins to be his agriculture secretary.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy ‘DOGE’ confidence in Supreme Court may be tested
item.description: Musk and Ramaswamy will head a nongovernmental commission to dismantle government bureaucracy and cut costs.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Bezos, Musk rivalry heats up ahead of Trump’s second term
item.description: A social media spat between Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos this week underscored a sharpening rivalry between two of the world’s richest men at a time when both are trying to grow their sprawling business empires in President-elect Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Driver in crash that killed 4 kids was allegedly photographing ‘Welcome to Florida’ sign
item.description: A New York man was extradited to Florida this month after he was accused of causing a deadly crash when he came to a near-stop on Interstate 95 to take photo of the "Welcome to Florida" sign.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WGN 9

item.title: Alabama carries out third nitrogen hypoxia execution in the country
item.description: Carey Dale Grayson, 50, fought his restraints and raised both middle fingers just before gas was administered, a witness said.
item.dc:creator: Asher Redd, WGN 9

item.title: Trump names Seb Gorka, Alex Wong to be senior national security staff
item.description: President-elect Trump has tapped his first term alums Alex Wong and Sebastian Gorka to join his national security team for his second go-around.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, NewsNation

item.title: Voters rejected historic election reforms across the US, despite more than $100M push
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — Two weeks before Election Day, activists from across the country gathered for an online rally heralding the historic number of state ballot initiatives seeking to change the way people vote.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Storm inundates Northern California with rain, heavy snow. Thousands remain in the dark in Seattle
item.description: HEALDSBURG, Calif. (AP) — Heavy downpours fell over much of Northern California on Friday, causing small landslides, overflowing a river and flooding some streets, including in parts of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: NOAH BERGER and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jelly Roll pays tribute to Oklahoma teen who died from fentanyl
item.description: The 19-year-old died from a fentanyl overdose in May 2023, while attending Cameron University. Rain, who thought she was taking a Xanax, turned out the pill was laced with fentanyl.
item.dc:creator: Mario Gonzalez/KFOR, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colorado funeral home owners who let nearly 190 bodies decay plead guilty to corpse abuse
item.description: COLORADO SPRINGS, Colo. (AP) — The owners of a Colorado funeral home who let nearly 190 bodies decay in a room-temperature building and gave grieving families fake ashes pleaded guilty on Friday to corpse abuse.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump taps NFL player Scott Turner as head of HUD
item.description: Turner serves as the chair of the Center for Education Opportunity at America First Policy Institute, a Trump-aligned think tank.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: McDonald's unveils new value menu, extends $5 Meal Deal
item.description: The fast-food giant announced on Friday that it will launch a new everyday value menu on Jan. 7, 2025, which will include exclusive in-app offers, a new Buy One, Add One offer, and the popular $5 Meal Deal.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Officer kills pet dog mistaken for a coyote in Massachusetts town. The owner says it was unnecessary
item.description: An animal control officer shot and killed a pet dog in a Massachusetts town after mistaking it for a coyote in an incident that has the animal's owner fuming, but that local police are describing as a sad mix-up.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: No ball at Tropicana Field in 2026 after Milton; new ballpark deal dies: Tampa Bay Rays
item.description: The St. Petersburg City Council has voted to spend over $23 million to repair Tropicana Field after Hurricane Milton destroyed the roof on the home of the Tampa Bay Rays.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, Fox 5

item.title: 3 Utah children missing since 2022 found living with FLDS Church, returned to mother
item.description: Three Utah children missing for two years have been located living with members of the Fundamentalist Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, according to the Fredonia Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McDonald's unveils new value menu, extends $5 Meal Deal
item.description: McDonald’s is offering its customers more ways to save on Big Macs, McNuggets, and other food items beginning in the New Year. The fast-food giant announced on Friday that it will launch a new everyday value menu on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Gaetz says he will not return to Congress
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) said Friday he will not return to Congress after resigning his House seat last week, despite having withdrawn from consideration to be President-elect Trump’s attorney general — but added he is “not going anywhere.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump immigration crackdown: Denaturalization just a drop in the bucket
item.description: President-elect Trump’s incoming administration is expected to take aim at legal immigration, in addition to cracking down on the illegal variety, slowing the pace of application approvals and redirecting resources to look for fraud in old applications, including naturalizations.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, Fox 5

item.title: American Airlines expands new software to improve boarding process
item.description: American Airlines is expanding new software to more than 100 airports to improve the boarding process.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Passengers duct tape unruly man who tried to open door on American Airlines plane
item.description: A group of passengers aboard a Dallas-bound flight restrained a man with duct tape after he claimed to be the "captain" and attempted to open the cabin door of an American Airlines plane midair, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump has promised again to release the last JFK files. But experts say don’t expect big revelations
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — More than 60 years after President John F. Kennedy was assassinated, conspiracy theories still swirl and any new glimpse into the fateful day of Nov. 22, 1963, in Dallas continues to fascinate.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal from AG nomination is among quickest in history
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew his nomination as President-elect Trump’s attorney general on Thursday, putting an end to a chaotic several days on Capitol Hill and making him one of the quickest in history to withdraw from Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Number of women who are state lawmakers inches up to a record high
item.description: Women will for the first time make up a majority of state legislators in Colorado and New Mexico next year, but at least 13 states saw losses in female representation after the November election, according to a count released Thursday by the Rutgers Center for American Women and Politics.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina official overseeing hurricane rebuilding efforts is no longer in role
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The top official for North Carolina's efforts to rebuild communities after natural disasters is no longer working in the position just a few days after a scathing rebuke of her work from GOP state lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A kayaker faked his own death and fled overseas. Now he's telling how he did it
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer so he could abandon his wife and three children has been communicating with authorities daily from Eastern Europe, even telling them how he did it, but has not committed to returning home, a sheriff said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kit Kats to come in new shape for the first time ever in America
item.description: The Hershey Company has debuted Kit Kats in the shape of something other than a traditional Kit Kat "finger.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, KRON4
